DECISION: Major Revision
FROM: Prof. Chris Forman, Department Editor, Management Science

Dear Authors,
The reports are outstanding and the AE report does an excellent job of summarizing the issues. I had similar questions as those raised by R1 and R2 about the source of variation that identifies the diff-in-diff.

-----------Associate Editor's text comments (if any)
Associate Editor
Comments to Author:
I highlight below the important elements that need to be covered in the next revision of the paper:
1 - Institutional and Natural Experiment Details
R2 highlights a number of questions and clarifications on the institutional details and the nature of the experiment.

2 - Estimation Approach
R1 and R2 raise several good points about the estimation approach used with the paper. I recommend that you start your analysis section with a discussion about feasible approaches and justify your choices.

3- Mechanism
All three referees are concerned about the mechanism you are advancing in the paper. I would encourage you to think deeply about the suggestions.

-----------Reviewers' text comments (if any)
Reviewer: 1
Comments to the Author
1.
Diff-in-diff versus cross-sectional regressions. The paper eventually addresses this issue amidst other robustness checks. I do feel that this point should at least be flagged much earlier.

2.
IV-regression? The regressions of contributions on a treatment dummy can be conceived as the reduced-form of an IV-regression that puts the results in more meaningful units.

Reviewer: 3
Comments to the Author
Major concerns:
1.
The mechanism is not so convincing at the moment. To directly test ownership, I think the author needs variation on each contributor's sense of ownership.

2.
Relatedly, in the control group, we need to have a better understanding of how contribution dynamics unfold.
